issues.title: Consider changing timestamps to milliseconds.
issues.author: dvorak42
issues.body:
While for most practical expirations, seconds should suffice, with a uint64, using milliseconds should also provide enough range for most practical purposes and it seems most other standards use milliseconds over seconds. (though I'm not sure if there's a hard and fast rule about which to use)

Given that the timestamp_precision already mitigates overly precise timestamps from being a privacy issue, would it make sense to change these to milliseconds?

comments.author: smhendrickson
comments.body:
I am supportive of the move to ms to provide more flexibility. Happy to make this change.

@chris-wood thoughts?

issues.title: "Expiration" vs "Time window"
issues.author: punkeel
issues.body:
Hi!

> Alternatively, Clients could pre-fetch tokens for the entire day all at once, including an expiration timestamp in each token to indicate the **time window** for which the token is valid.

Nitpick questions because this isn't entirely clear to me.

1. Is there a well-known sort of mechanism to help Clients "know" what precision they should use? Does it need to be negotiated/learned from the issuer?

2. The given example is NOT _exactly_ mapped into the proposal, namely: in one case, the Client gets one token per hour, in the other they may get 24 tokens per day. The _big_ difference being the Client can decide to redeem ALL their tokens in a **burst**.

   The words "time window" imply there may be a lower AND upper bound (`[nbf, exp]` vs `[now, exp]`). Was this considered?

3. If a Client fetches 24 tokens, is this done in a batch? If yes: this may allow the issuer to validate the tokens don't overlap (-> burst). Should this behavior be documented/recommended?

4. I'm not clear how this impacts token redemption - is the check `now() < timestamp`? `now() < timestamp ± timestamp_period`? `now() < timestamp + timestamp_period`?

    If it's just `now() < timestamp`, is the period needed in the token itself?

comments.author: smhendrickson
comments.body:
Thank you for the interest! Sorry for the late reply.

There is no effective way for a verifier to take action on time window data, because it cannot associate n requests at the redemption stage. All it can know is if now() < timestamp. In fact, it could implement its own policy (e.g. window of x on other side of timestamp).

I think the token issuance policy needs to accommodate for acceptable burstyness. If the policy allows a client to sit on so many tokens that the redeemer can't handle the max burst, the issuance policy likely needs be adjusted.

If you have suggestions on how to phrase this in the draft, I'd be happy to review. Thanks again.

pulls.title: ci: upgrade upload-artifact from v3 to v4
pulls.author: copilot-swe-agent
pulls.body:
GitHub now hard-fails jobs using `actions/upload-artifact@v3`. The `Publish New Draft Version` workflow was blocked at the archive step.

## Changes
- **`.github/workflows/publish.yml`**: Replace deprecated `upload-artifact@v3` with `@v4`; add explicit `name: submitted-drafts` (required by v4)

```diff
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
+ uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
+   name: submitted-drafts
    path: "versioned/draft-*-[0-9][0-9].*"
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Fix the failing GitHub Actions workflow in repository `ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension`.
> 
> Context:
> - The failing job is from workflow `.github/workflows/publish.yml`.
> - The failure message is: `This request has been automatically failed because it uses a deprecated version of actions/upload-artifact: v3`.
> - The failing step is the final artifact archival step in the `Publish New Draft Version` workflow.
> - Current workflow file at commit `f0c8f5654230f9e3a628bda6160f406c9110c932` uses `actions/upload-artifact@v3` around lines 46-49.
> 
> Requested change:
> - Update the workflow to use a supported version of `actions/upload-artifact`.
> - Prefer the minimal safe change needed to unblock the workflow.
> - Preserve the current workflow behavior unless a small compatibility adjustment is required by v4.
> 
> Relevant file:
> - `.github/workflows/publish.yml`
> 
> Acceptance criteria:
> - The workflow no longer uses `actions/upload-artifact@v3`.
> - The workflow remains valid YAML.
> - The `Publish New Draft Version` job should be able to run without failing due to the deprecated artifact action.
> - Keep the change narrowly scoped to fixing this failure unless another directly related adjustment is necessary.
> 
> The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):
> 
> User: Please find a solution for this failing job. Use the logs, job definition, and any referenced files where the failure occurred. Keep your response focused on the solution and include code suggestions when appropriate.
> 
> Assistant: The failure is caused by a deprecated GitHub Action, not by the draft build itself.
> 
> ### Root cause
> The job log shows:
> 
> - `This request has been automatically failed because it uses a deprecated version of actions/upload-artifact: v3`
> 
> That matches the workflow definition in [`.github/workflows/publish.yml`](https://github.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension/blob/f0c8f5654230f9e3a628bda6160f406c9110c932/.github/workflows/publish.yml#L46-L49):
> 
> ```yaml
> - name: "Archive Submitted Drafts"
>   uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
>   with:
>     path: "versioned/draft-*-[0-9][0-9].*"
> ```
> 
> ### Fix
> Update the workflow to use `actions/upload-artifact@v4`.
> 
> Suggested patch for [`.github/workflows/publish.yml`](https://github.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension/blob/f0c8f5654230f9e3a628bda6160f406c9110c932/.github/workflows/publish.yml#L46-L49):
> 
> ```yaml
> - name: "Archive Submitted Drafts"
>   uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
>   with:
>     path: "versioned/draft-*-[0-9][0-9].*"
> ```
> 
> ### Recommended full updated section
> ```yaml
> - name: "Archive Submitted Drafts"
>   uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
>   with:
>     name: submitted-drafts
>     path: "versioned/draft-*-[0-9][0-9].*"
>     if-no-files-found: error
> ```
> 
> ### Why this solves it
> GitHub is now blocking `upload-artifact@v3`, so the job fails before the publishing flow can complete. Switching to `@v4` removes the deprecated dependency and allows the archive step to run.
> 
> ### Notes on related files
> The failure is in the workflow itself, not in the `Makefile`. The `Makefile` only bootstraps `lib/main.mk` and does not reference artifact upload behavior:
> [`Makefile`](https://github.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension/blob/f0c8f5654230f9e3a628bda6160f406c9110c932/Makefile#L1-L11)
> 
> ### Minimal change
> If you want the smallest safe fix, change only this line:
> 
> ```diff
> -      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
> +      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
> ```
> 
> User: Draft a PR to fix it
> 
> Assistant: I drafted the PR request to fix the workflow by replacing the deprecated artifact action with the supported version in `.github/workflows/publish.yml`.
> 
> Proposed PR title:
> **Update publish workflow to use upload-artifact v4**
> 
> Proposed change:
> ```diff
> -      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
> +      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
> ```
> 
> Suggested final workflow step:
> ```yaml
> - name: "Archive Submitted Drafts"
>   uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
>   with:
>     path: "versioned/draft-*-[0-9][0-9].*"
> ```
>

pulls.title: ci: upgrade actions/upload-artifact from v3 to v4
pulls.author: copilot-swe-agent
pulls.body:
GitHub deprecated `actions/upload-artifact@v3`, causing the **Update Editor's Copy** workflow to fail immediately — before any build steps run.

## Change

- `.github/workflows/ghpages.yml`: `actions/upload-artifact@v3` → `actions/upload-artifact@v4` in the `Archive Built Drafts` step

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

Fix the failing GitHub Actions workflow in repository `ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension`.

A workflow run failed in job `76053213419` from run `25878873308` for workflow `.github/workflows/ghpages.yml` (`Update Editor's Copy`). The failure occurs before the build runs because the workflow uses a deprecated artifact action version:

- `actions/upload-artifact@v3`

The log reports:
- "This request has been automatically failed because it uses a deprecated version of `actions/upload-artifact: v3`."

Required fix:
- Update `.github/workflows/ghpages.yml` so the `Archive Built Drafts` step uses `actions/upload-artifact@v4` instead of `@v3`.

Suggested scope:
- Make the minimal safe change needed to restore the workflow.
- Verify whether any adjacent workflow action version updates are necessary; if not necessary, keep the PR narrowly scoped.

Acceptance criteria:
- The workflow file no longer references `actions/upload-artifact@v3` in the failing step.
- The PR is limited to the workflow fix needed to address the deprecation failure.
- Create a pull request with a clear title and summary describing the artifact action upgrade.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: Please find a solution for this failing job. Use the logs, job definition, and any referenced files where the failure occurred. Keep your response focused on the solution and include code suggestions when appropriate.

Assistant: The failure is caused by a deprecated GitHub Action in the workflow, not by the draft build itself.

### Root cause
The job fails immediately with:

> This request has been automatically failed because it uses a deprecated version of `actions/upload-artifact: v3`.

From the workflow file at [`.github/workflows/ghpages.yml`](https://github.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension/blob/9ee1e5bdf7a26fb0cb5419eb5318a801f2bc1ce6/.github/workflows/ghpages.yml#L53-L58), the failing step is:

```yaml
- name: "Archive Built Drafts"
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
  with:
    path: |
      draft-*.html
      draft-*.txt
```

### Fix
Update that action to `v4`.

Suggested patch:

```yaml
- name: "Archive Built Drafts"
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    path: |
      draft-*.html
      draft-*.txt
```

### File to change
[` .github/workflows/ghpages.yml `](https://github.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension/blob/9ee1e5bdf7a26fb0cb5419eb5318a801f2bc1ce6/.github/workflows/ghpages.yml#L53-L58)

### Optional cleanup
The same workflow also uses older major versions of other GitHub-maintained actions:

- [`actions/checkout@v3`](https://github.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension/blob/9ee1e5bdf7a26fb0cb5419eb5318a801f2bc1ce6/.github/workflows/ghpages.yml#L22-L23)
- [`actions/cache@v3`](https://github.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-ietf-privacypass-expiration-extension/blob/9ee1e5bdf7a26fb0cb5419eb5318a801f2bc1ce6/.github/workflows/ghpages.yml#L29-L39)

Those are not the reported failure here, but updating them to `v4` as well would keep the workflow current:

```yaml
- name: "Checkout"
  uses: actions/checkout@v4

- name: "Caching"
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      .refcache
      .venv
      .gems
      node_modules
      .targets.mk
    key: i-d-${{ steps.setup.outputs.date }}
    restore-keys: i-d-
```

### Recommended final workflow change
```yaml
name: "Update Editor's Copy"

on:
  push:
    paths-ignore:
    - README.md
    - CONTRIBUTING.md
    - LICENSE.md
    - .gitignore
  pull_request:
    paths-ignore:
    - README.md
    - CONTRIBUTING.md
    - LICENSE.md
    - .gitignore

jobs:
  build:
    name: "Update Editor's Copy"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - name: "Checkout"
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: "Setup"
      id: setup
      run: date -u "+date=%FT%T" >>"$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

    - name: "Caching"
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: |
          .refcache
          .venv
          .gems
          node_modules
          .targets.mk
        key: i-d-${{ steps.setup.outputs.date }}
        restore-keys: i-d-

    - name: "Build Drafts"
      uses: martinthomson/i-d-template@v1
      with:
        token: ${{ github.token }}

    - name: "Update GitHub Pages"
      uses: martinthomson/i-d-template@v1
      if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' }}
      with:
        make: gh-pages
        token: ${{ github.token }}

    - name: "Archive Built Drafts"
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        path: |
          draft-*.html
          draft-*.txt
```

The minimal required fix is just changing `actions/upload-artifact@v3` to `@v4`.

User: Fix it

Assistant: I’m waiting on the repository action you were prompted to approve or reject; once you respond, I can continue with the fix.

User: @Copilot Accepted Confirmation: Are you sure?

Assistant: I can’t complete the f...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

*This pull request was created from Copilot chat.*
